# Standard Indicative Budgetary Quotation Template

## Document structure

All indicative budgetary quotations follow this structure for consistency, traceability, and to ensure they cannot be mistaken for a binding offer.

### Cover page

- Customer name and reference
- Ericsson opportunity reference
- Issue date
- Validity period (typically 60 days for indicative quotations)
- Clear marking: "INDICATIVE BUDGETARY QUOTATION — NON-BINDING"

### Section 1: Executive summary

- One-page overview of the proposed solution
- Headline commercial figures (indicative range, not point estimates)
- Strategic alignment statement
- Indicative timeline

### Section 2: Solution description

- High-level technical description tied to customer requirements
- Reference to relevant Ericsson product portfolio
- Indicative integration with customer's installed base
- Notes on software, services, and support scope
- **Not** a detailed bill of materials at indicative stage; broad equipment categories with quantities

### Section 3: Statement of compliance

- Point-by-point response to customer requirements
- Compliance status for each: FULL / PARTIAL (with caveat) / NON-COMPLIANT (with explanation) / OPTIONAL (additional cost)
- Confidence indicator: confirmed / subject to detailed design / subject to site survey

### Section 4: Pricing schedule

- Equipment categories with indicative pricing (ranges, not point estimates)
- Software, by license type
- Services, by category (network design, installation, integration, project management, training)
- Support and warranty by tier
- Commercial construct options (one-time, multi-year framework, software-attach incentive)
- Indicative discount levels (if any)
- Note that pricing is subject to firm scope and firm commercial discussion

### Section 5: Indicative commercial construct

- Recommended commercial structure given the customer's profile and requirements
- Multi-year commitment options with associated benefits
- Software-attach options
- Payment and indexation indicative position
- Note: terms are subject to formal contract negotiation

### Section 6: Deployment timeline

- Indicative phasing (Q1/Q2/Q3 phases, not specific dates)
- Critical path items
- Dependencies on customer-side activities

### Section 7: Assumptions and caveats

- All technical assumptions (site availability, power, transport, integration scope)
- All commercial assumptions (currency, payment timing, volume, framework agreement applicability)
- Clear statement that final pricing requires:
  - Detailed scope definition
  - Site surveys for confirmed dimensioning
  - Firm commercial discussion
  - Final approval flow within Ericsson

### Section 8: Next steps

- Recommended next steps to move from indicative to firm offer
- Engagement contacts on both sides
- Indicative timeline for firm offer process

---

## Style guidelines

- Customer-facing language: clear, direct, no Ericsson internal jargon
- Avoid speculative claims about technical capabilities not in our confirmed roadmap
- Always state ranges, never point estimates at indicative stage
- Always flag dependencies on customer activities
- Never imply binding intent

## What to avoid

- Detailed bill of materials at indicative stage (locks technical position prematurely)
- Specific discount percentages at indicative stage (sets reference for firm negotiation)
- Specific terms and conditions at indicative stage (terms are for formal negotiation)
- Any statement that could be interpreted as an offer to be bound
